question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the execute() endpoint with concurrent contract call requests, each consuming maximum gas, causing the executor.execute() call at line 92 to queue and stall, delaying block finalization by 500% or more of the 24-hour average block time and temporarily freezing network transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged user submit a contract call with an artificially inflated estimatedGas value to buildContractCallTransactionBody(), causing executor.execute() to run an unbounded EVM computation loop that stalls the block pipeline for 500%+ of the average block time, effectively freezing pending transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: buildEthereumTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged user craft an EthereumTransactionBody with maxGasAllowance set to Long.MAX_VALUE (line 205) and submit it via the debug endpoint, causing the executor to attempt to consume near-infinite gas during block execution, stalling block production by 500%+ of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: defaultTransactionBodyBuilder()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that transactionValidStart is set to Instant.now().getEpochSecond() (line 166) with zero nanoseconds, causing timestamp collisions across concurrent requests that force the executor to serialize block processing, delaying a block by 500%+ of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged user submit a ContractDebugParameters request with a large ethereumData payload that triggers buildEthereumTransactionBody() followed by patchSenderNonce(), causing the ContractCallContext state write operations to block the shared executor thread pool and delay block finalization by 500%+ of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: patchSenderNonce()] Can an unprivileged user repeatedly call the debug trace endpoint with crafted ethereumData whose nonce differs from the stored account nonce, causing patchSenderNonce() to continuously write to the ContractCallContext writable cache, saturating the state write path and delaying block processing by 500%+ of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged user trigger the isContractCreate branch (params.getReceiver().isZero()) with a maximally-sized initcode payload in buildContractCreateTransactionBody(), causing the EVM to execute a deployment that runs for 500%+ of the average block time, blocking all subsequent transactions in the block queue?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the synchronous nature of executor.execute(transactionBody, Instant.now(), getOperationTracers()) at line 92 by submitting thousands of concurrent requests, exhausting the thread pool that services block production and causing a block delay of 500%+ of the average block time?
